text:
One afternoon, young Tom wandered into Mrs. Jenkins' flower garden. He was curious about everything he could see; tulips of every color, roses in vibrant shades, and tiny butterflies flitting from bloom to bloom. Mrs. Jenkins welcomed him, smiling at his wide eyes.

"Come in, Tom," she said warmly. “Pick some flowers and put them in my vase. My tulips especially need some extra attention.”

Tom selected a few flowers from different patches and placed them in the vase without a word. He felt a sense of peace among the fragrant petals.

Later that evening, with the sun dipping below the horizon, casting deep reds across the sky, Tom lay awake thinking about his day. As he turned onto his side, he noticed the vase by his pillow, still breathing softly.

"That was nice," he murmured. But his thoughts wandered to adventures yet to come. Maybe he could climb Mrs. Jenkins’ apple tree, or learn how to juggle. His
